select_when: A team needs to separate competing modes of thought so risk review, creativity, evidence checking, and synthesis do not jam each other.
Meetings are stuck in unstructured argument and would benefit from deliberate perspective rotation.
The same group must examine one issue from several angles without mistaking the first dominant frame for the whole problem.
Role separation would reduce cognitive load and improve objectivity in a complex review.
avoid_when: The roles are becoming performative labels and participants have stopped doing the real cognitive work of switching standards and evidence.
The frame rotation is adding theater without improving what gets checked, challenged, or synthesized.

curation_notes.summary: Focused on structured perspective rotation and anti-argument-jam discipline rather than on role theater.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor agent-architecture sprawl because the raw source is already strongest on structured human review modes.
open_questions: The source overlaps with agent design, but the operational file stays on multi-angle human review.

select_when.note: The raw source is explicit on separating thought modes, unstructured meetings, and deliberate perspective rotation.

avoid_when.note: The performative-role warning is explicit; the second line is reviewed normalization of the same misuse.

input_type.note: The typed label compresses the raw condition of argument-jammed group thinking.

output_type.note: The output label compresses structured perspective rotation into a reusable review type.

reasoning_types.note: The model is reflective and system-organizing, but the taxonomy labels remain reviewed normalization.
